preview_text:
The model favors Spain at 91%, assigning Cape Verde a win probability so low it rounds to zero. The sportsbook consensus is slightly more generous to the underdog, placing Spain at 88% and Cape Verde at 4%, a meaningful 3–4 percentage-point gap that reflects broader market caution around historically extreme forecasts.

The expected match shape centers on a Spanish victory, but the draw carries genuine plausibility at 9%. The narrow margin between win and draw probabilities suggests Spain is heavily favored without being overwhelming on every possible outcome. A Cape Verde win remains a remote tail risk in both model and market, though the sportsbooks assign it noticeably more probability than the model's near-zero estimate.

Model and sportsbook consensus are closely aligned on the headline outcome—both place Spain well above 85%—but they diverge on the lower tail. The model concentrates probability mass almost entirely on Spain or a draw, while the market allocates a small but visible slice to Cape Verde. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these approaches; the gap may reflect genuine uncertainty about underdog tail risk or model calibration in asymmetric matchups.
